item.title: U.S. restarts blockade and strikes Iran as Trump seeks control of Strait of Hormuz
item.description: As the U.S. restarts its Iran blockade and carries out more strikes, President Trump has called off a planned 20% fee on shipping through the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: After ICE fatally shot multiple people, officials speak out: "We want them out"
item.description: U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement officials shot and killed two men in the last week in Maine and in Texas. Maine's House speaker, a Houston prosecutor and a Minnesota attorney spoke with CBS News about the violence.

item.title: Houston prosecutor could bring charges against ICE agents in fatal shooting
item.description: Harris County District Attorney Sean Teare, who is investigating the fatal ICE shooting of Lorenzo Salgado Araujo, told CBS News ICE's tactics "in no way resemble" the behavior of police agencies he's worked with.

item.title: Anthropic launches "Claude for Teachers," giving educators a year of free access to AI tools
item.description: Anthropic is offering verified teachers a year of free access to its premium AI tools through its new "Claude for Teachers" program. Elizabeth Kelly, head of Beneficial Deployments at Anthopic, joins CBS News to discuss.

item.title: Houston prosecutor says he's trying to get visas for witnesses of fatal ICE shooting
item.description: Harris County District Attorney Sean Teare, who is investigating the ICE killing of Mexican immigrant Lorenzo Salgado Araujo, says his office is filing paperwork to get visas for the witnesses of the fatal shooting, so they're not deported while the investigation unfolds.

item.title: Utility companies requested $9.2 billion in rate increases during second quarter, report shows
item.description: Utility companies requested $9.2 billion worth of rate increases in the second quarter of 2026, potentially impacting over 56 million Americans, according to a new report from PowerLines. Former Michigan utility commissioner Tremaine Phillips joins CBS News to discuss.

item.title: Security video captures moments after man was fatally shot by ICE in Maine
item.description: A home security camera captured the sound of five gunshots when Johan Sebastián Durán Guerrero, a Colombian national, was fatally shot by an Immigration and Customs Enforcement officer.

item.title: NASA astronaut joins two Russians on trip to International Space Station for 8-month stay
item.description: Physician-astronaut Anil Menon, a former flight surgeon with NASA, rocketed into space Tuesday with two Russian cosmonauts for an eight-month stay aboard the International Space Station. CBS News' space consultant Bill Harwood has more.

item.title: How Jeffrey Epstein earned a $25 million payout through a bank's DOJ settlement
item.description: Documents show that convicted sex offender Jeffrey Epstein earned a $25 million fee for helping Swiss bank Edmond de Rothschild resolve a Justice Department investigation. CBS News' Katrina Kaufman reports.

item.title: Trump says he won't rule out sending ground troops to Iran
item.description: President Trump told Fox News on Tuesday that he won't rule out a ground invasion of Iran. The comments come amid another round of U.S. strikes against Iranian targets. CBS News' Eleanor Watson reports.

item.title: Spain defeats France 2-0 to reach FIFA World Cup final
item.description: Spain emerged triumphant from its World Cup semifinal on Tuesday, beating France 2-0 and securing a spot in the final, which will be played in New Jersey on Sunday. CBS News' Nicole Valdes reports.

item.title: Cyclospora outbreak leaves consumers guessing which foods are safe
item.description: Officials are still searching for the source of the outbreak, prompting consumers to seek advice on social media about which foods to avoid.

item.title: ICE halts most vehicle stops following deadly shootings, sources say
item.description: Neither of the victims of the ICE shootings in Maine or Texas were the target of enforcement operations, according to the Department of Homeland Security.

item.title: The 2026 FIFA World Cup schedule and how to watch
item.description: With 104 World Cup games being played in the U.S., Canada and Mexico, it's like "a Super Bowl every single day for five weeks," U.S. team captain Tim Ream told CBS News.

item.title: Spain reaches World Cup final with dominant 2-0 win over France
item.description: In a match Tuesday featuring two of soccer's biggest heavyweights, Spain put in a masterful performance, frustrating France to the tune of a 2-0 win to advance to Sunday's World Cup final​.

item.title: Supreme Court Justices Barrett and Kagan plead with congress for $230 million security budget
item.description: Supreme Court Justices Amy Coney Barrett and Elena Kagan appeared before House and Senate committees Tuesday to argue for an increase to their security budget, testifying on the increasing threats they face. CBS News' Taurean Small reports.

item.title: Granddaughter wears late grandmother's wedding dress with no changes: "Why would I fix perfection?"
item.description: The eldest granddaughter in a Pennsylvania woman's family long wanted to wear her grandmother's dress for her own wedding. When the dress fit perfectly, Catherine Michelle Bartlett walked down the aisle in it, exactly as is. Tony Dokoupil has the story.

item.title: U.S. launches new attacks on Iran as naval blockade begins
item.description: The U.S. military launched a new wave of strikes against Iran Tuesday as it reinstated its naval blockade against the country. CBS News' Olivia Rinaldi reports.

item.title: State board: Musk may have broken election law by offering $1M checks to voters
item.description: The Wisconsin Elections Commission found billionaire Elon Musk may have violated the state's election bribery law by offering $1 million checks to voters during an election last year.

item.title: Torrential rain triggers flash flood emergency in Texas
item.description: Heavy storms caused chaos in Central Texas this week, triggering a flash flood emergency, and more than a foot of rain swelled rivers. Jason Allen reports and Rob Marciano has the forecast.

item.title: The politics of New York's data center construction pause
item.description: New York has enacted a statewide pause on the construction of new data centers for one year. Political strategists Chuck Rocha and Matt Whitlock discuss.

item.title: ICE ordered to suspend most vehicle stops following lethal shootings in Texas and Maine
item.description: ICE agents have been ordered to suspend most vehicle stops following two deadly shootings involving the agency this week in Texas and Maine. CBS News' Lilia Luciano and Camilo Montoya-Galvez report.

item.title: Spain moves on to World Cup final after defeating France on a national holiday
item.description: Spain advanced to the World Cup final on Tuesday, after stunning France on the country's most patriotic national holiday. Nicole Valdes has the recap.

item.title: 7/14: CBS Evening News
item.description: More than 20 inches of rain are forecasted for parts of Texas; Immigration and Customs Enforcement changes its tactics after deadly shootings.

item.title: House passes bill to make daylight saving time permanent
item.description: The measure also allows states to opt out if they take action before the federal law is enacted.

item.title: 7/14: The Takeout with Major Garrett
item.description: Latest on ICE scrutiny after fatal shootings in Texas and Maine; aide to Rep. Ro Khanna on moment he says he was detained in the West Bank.

item.title: Supreme Court pushes Congress for more security: "Threats have come very close"
item.description: Supreme Court Justices Elena Kagan and Amy Coney Barrett urged Congress to provide additional funding to enhance protection for the justices as they face a rise in threats.

item.title: My Mother's Murder Trials - Part 2
item.description: A daughter suspects her mother of a double murder. Years later, courtroom drama brings unsettling answers. CBS News chief correspondent Jim Axelrod reports for "48 Hours."

item.title: My Mother's Murder Trials - Part 1
item.description: A daughter suspects her mother of a double murder. Years later, courtroom drama brings unsettling answers. CBS News chief correspondent Jim Axelrod reports for "48 Hours."

item.title: What to know about "explosive" diarrhea-causing parasite reported in several states
item.description: Michigan is experiencing its largest outbreak of a parasitic infection, called cyclosporiasis, that causes severe diarrhea.
